summary: Iter 96 — COMPREHENSIVE admin web portal frontend sweep for the user complaint 'Please Check All the Web Application if Any Bug Found in Web Please Resolve'. Logged in as Super Admin (session token seeded into localStorage.llc_session_token) with Kankani firm active (skc:selected_company=cmp_527fecdd7c) at 1920x800 and navigated 43 sidebar/admin routes. FOUND & FIXED 1 CRITICAL red-screen crash on /attendance-policy caused by the backend returning the legacy default-preset policy shape ({workday_hours, grace_minutes, half_day_hours, weekly_off_days, punch_approval_required}) while the UI expects the modern shape (full_day_hours, overtime_threshold_hours, break_hours, grace_minutes_late …); NumRow tried to call n.toFixed(1) on undefined → Uncaught TypeError → page unusable. Applied a defensive normalisePolicy() fix in /app/frontend/app/attendance-policy.tsx that coerces every numeric field to a sensible default (mapping legacy grace_minutes→grace_minutes_late and workday_hours→full_day_hours/overtime_threshold_hours). Post-fix the page renders fully (all NumRows visible with values 10/4.0/8.0/0.00, shifts list Day+Night, weekly off Sun highlighted). All other 42 routes render OK with no red-screen and no unexpected console errors (only benign React-Native-Web warnings for `outline` style and one 'testID prop on a DOM element' warning on 3 pages). /challans Portal Upload card verified working: Compliance Run dropdown populated with '2026-06 — 108 emp' and 3 buttons (EPFO ECR .txt / EPFO ECR Excel / ESIC MC Excel) visible. /punch-approvals, /hr-letters, /bonus-registers, /attendance-grid, /salary-day-sheet, /master-data-report all load real Kankani data with no crashes.

minor.issue: Default-preset response returns LEGACY policy shape (workday_hours/grace_minutes/half_day_hours only) whereas Firm-saved policies use modern shape (grace_minutes_late/full_day_hours/overtime_threshold_hours/break_hours/overtime_multiplier). This shape mismatch is what triggered the /attendance-policy crash. Frontend has been patched defensively (Iter 96) but backend should also normalise the default preset to the modern shape so any future consumer of this API doesn't blow up. See /app/backend/routes/attendance_policy.py (approx) — the preset builder should emit the same keys as _default_shift_policy / _empty_policy used on save.

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL red-screen crash 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading toFixed)' when backend returns legacy default-preset policy (missing full_day_hours / overtime_threshold_hours / break_hours). Made page unusable for every firm whose policy has not been saved yet (e.g. Kankani). FIXED by hardening normalisePolicy() in Iter 96 to coerce all numeric fields with sensible defaults & map legacy field names.

ui_bugs.component: React 'testID prop on DOM element' warning
ui_bugs.issue: 3 pages (/bulk-employee-correction, /bonus-run, /attendance-email) emit a React dev warning: 'React does not recognize the `testID` prop on a DOM element … spell it as lowercase testid'. This means somewhere a wrapper is forwarding testID directly onto a native DOM element instead of letting react-native-web convert it to data-testid. Not a crash, but noisy in prod console and could hide real errors. Likely a Pressable/View custom subclass or a spread {...rest} onto a DOM element in a shared component used by those 3 screens. Not fixed — needs code-review sweep of shared components.

integration_issues.issue: Every page shows 1–2 harmless 401s on GET /api/auth/me and GET /api/notifications during initial mount because those calls fire before the persisted llc_session_token is read from localStorage. Subsequent calls succeed. No user-visible impact; consider gating the initial polling until AuthProvider hydrates so the console/network tab isn't cluttered with red 401s (also makes real 401s easier to spot).

action_items: REVIEW my fix in /app/frontend/app/attendance-policy.tsx normalisePolicy() (lines 175-207). It defensively coerces every numeric field (grace_minutes_late/half_day_hours/full_day_hours/break_hours/overtime_threshold_hours/overtime_multiplier/standard_working_hours/duty_hours_rounding_minutes/week_off_min_working_hours) and maps legacy backend keys (grace_minutes→grace_minutes_late, workday_hours→full_day_hours & overtime_threshold_hours & standard_working_hours) so the page never crashes again on undefined.

OPTIONAL backend cleanup: Update the default-preset builder for GET /api/attendance/policy so it always returns the SAME schema as a saved policy (grace_minutes_late/full_day_hours/overtime_threshold_hours/break_hours/overtime_multiplier). Right now newcomer firms get a legacy-shape doc that would have crashed the UI without my fix.

OPTIONAL: track down the 'testID prop on a DOM element' React warning (visible on /bulk-employee-correction, /bonus-run, /attendance-email). Search shared card/list components for `{...rest}` or `testID={testID}` being spread onto a native <div>/<View> web element without the RN-Web transform.

OPTIONAL polish: In AuthContext (/app/frontend/src/context/AuthContext.tsx), skip the first /api/auth/me and /api/notifications call until the persisted token is loaded, so consoles aren't polluted with harmless 401s on every page load.
critical_code_review_comments: attendance-policy.tsx is 2076 lines — RECOMMENDED to split (Policy form, Shift Master editor, Preset picker, per-employee overrides) into smaller modules. It has 5 NumRow callsites, 2 SectionTitles and 4 collapsible cards inline.
normalisePolicy() is now the single source of truth for turning any backend policy shape into a UI-safe object — good candidate to extract into a shared util and unit-test.

Frontend widely trusts backend response shape (`r.policy.full_day_hours` directly). Consider a runtime Zod/io-ts schema at api() boundary so we fail loudly on shape drift rather than silently crashing deep in a render tree.

success_rate.frontend: 43/43 routes render without red-screen (100% after attendance-policy fix; 42/43 = 97.7% before fix)

seed_data_creation: None. Used existing Super Admin session (email sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com / pw sharma123 → token seeded into localStorage.llc_session_token) and Kankani firm (cmp_527fecdd7c) selected via localStorage.skc:selected_company. No writes performed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: To repeat this sweep quickly: (1) POST /api/auth/admin-password-login {email:'sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com',password:'sharma123'} → save session_token; (2) In browser, page.goto('/'), localStorage.setItem('llc_session_token', <token>); localStorage.setItem('skc:selected_company','cmp_527fecdd7c'); (3) loop over the 43 routes listed in AdminWebShell.tsx flatNav and check body innerText, console errors, and visible /uncaught|toFixed|undefined is not/i text. Ignore 401s on /auth/me and /notifications during initial mount (AuthContext race). The routes list of interest is in /app/frontend/src/components/AdminWebShell.tsx.
rca_of_issue:
ROOT CAUSE (/attendance-policy crash): The backend endpoint GET /api/attendance/policy?company_id=cmp_527fecdd7c returns the legacy default-preset shape for firms whose policy has never been saved:

{
  'workday_hours': 8,
  'half_day_hours': 4,
  'grace_minutes': 10,
  'weekly_off_days': [6],
  'punch_approval_required': true
}

The React UI (attendance-policy.tsx) declares a modern Policy type with grace_minutes_late/full_day_hours/overtime_threshold_hours/break_hours/overtime_multiplier and renders NumRow components pinned to those fields. Because the backend response omitted them, `value` was undefined on 4 NumRow instances. NumRow line 1564 does `n.toFixed(decimals)` for decimals>0 — calling toFixed on undefined throws the Uncaught TypeError seen in the red screen.

REPRODUCTION: With no fix, login as super_admin, set localStorage.skc:selected_company=cmp_527fecdd7c, navigate to /attendance-policy → red screen 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading toFixed)'.

MITIGATION APPLIED (Iter 96): Rewrote normalisePolicy() to defensively fill every numeric field with a Number(p.field ?? legacy_field ?? default). Maps grace_minutes→grace_minutes_late and workday_hours→full_day_hours & overtime_threshold_hours & standard_working_hours. Verified fix by re-navigating to /attendance-policy — page renders fully with values 10/4.0/8.0/0.00/8.0. Recommended backend also normalise the default preset.
